OF course, there Will be a police
ratron-not one, but three.


A city council that would have re-
sisted the stirring pleas made be.
fore 1t last night by the ladies OF
the Woman's club would have been
t1ard-hearted indeed. And further
more, the women, through their
spokesman, Mrs. p. p. Stafford,
said they had the "dope" that indi-
Gated the need Of the matron and
would spring 1t unless the council
granted the request for the matron.
Furthermore, Mrs. Stafford told
the city council what she thought
Of their body as whole, called
them lot of turncoats, change-of.
front men, and said that she had
heard that some of them wore a
dollar sign, back of which was the
Washington Water Power company,
the Spokesman-Review and other
forces.


She chided the councilmen for be-
ing led around by the nose by Chief
of Police Sullivan. and asked:


'Who is this man Sullivan, any,
way, that you have to consult his
wishes in preference to what Is
wanted by thousands of taxpay-
ers?"


Mrs. Stafford ripped up the police
department from stem to stern, and
said that she had learned from UNI
questioned sources that women
prisoners were mistreated within
the walls Of the jail, and it was not
from the I. w. WIS, either.


II may be arrested for criminal
conspiracy in the morning for say.
ing this. but l am not afraid" she
remarked.


The council tried to head off
Mrs. Stafford by interfering re-
